videoDescription:

By the end, you will have a deep understanding of the concepts and a great overall big picture of how Docker is used in the whole software development process.

videoTitle: Expert Talk: DevOps & Software Architecture • Simon Brown, Dave Farley & Hannes Lowette • GOTO 2021
videoDescription:
This interview was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen for GOTO Unscripted. #GOTOcon #GOTOunscripted #GOTOcph
https://gotopia.tech

Read the full transcription of this interview here:
https://gotopia.tech/articles/expert-talk-devops-software-architecture

Simon Brown - Author of "Software Architecture for Developers" & Creator of the C4 Software Architecture Model @simonbrown4821 
Dave Farley - Author of "Modern Software Engineering" Continuous Delivery & DevOps Pioneer @ModernSoftwareEngineeringYT 
Hannes Lowette - Head of Learning & Development at Axxes @Belenar82 

RESOURCES
https://youtu.be/D43PlUr1x_E • Alan Kay • GOTO 2021
https://youtu.be/lLnsi522LS8 • Mary Shaw • GOTO 2015
https://youtu.be/5OjqD-ow8GE • Simon Brown • GOTO 2018

DESCRIPTION
As software architecture continues to evolve rapidly, we are constantly confronted with new challenges. Simon Brown, Dave Farley and Hannes Lowette cover some of the recent trends in software architecture touching on terms such as DevOps and how to deal with complexity. They also reference concepts that have stirred debates forever and are still not done right, like bounded context and continuous delivery.

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
00:53 Evolution in software architecture
06:16 Managing complexity
14:22 Bounded context
22:00 Problems in our industry
25:00 Continuous delivery done right
28:56 DevOps: just a title?
31:47 Similarities between software architecture & architecture
39:23 Outro

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
Simon Brown • Software Architecture for Developers Vol. 2 • https://leanpub.com/visualising-software-architecture
David Farley • Modern Software Engineering • https://amzn.to/3GI468M
Dave Farley & Jez Humble • Continuous Delivery • https://amzn.to/3ocIHwd

videoTitle: Docker Tutorial for Beginners - A Full DevOps Course on How to Run Applications in Containers
videoDescription:
Get started using Docker with this end-to-end beginners course with hands-on labs.

Docker is an open platform for developers and sysadmins to build, ship, and run distributed applications, whether on laptops, data center VMs, or the cloud.

In this course you will learn Docker through a series of lectures that use animation, illustration and some fun analogies that simply complex concepts, we have demos that will show how to install and get started with Docker and most importantly we have hands-on labs that you can access right in your browser.

videoTitle: What is DevOps? - In Simple English
videoDescription:
Simple explanation of DevOps without the complications and information overload.  -- what do you think? Please share & comment! 

DevOps is a concept with different interpretations and definitions, but when you get down to it, it's all about developers and operations teams breaking down silos and working together to innovate faster. For many companies, the ability to innovate at a rapid pace — responding to market conditions and customer feedback — is a key factor for success. Adopting the DevOps philosophy requires a new mindset, new tools and new skills.

More about DevOps:

videoTitle: DevOps Engineering Course for Beginners
videoDescription:
What is DevOps in the context of software engineering? Learn all about DevOps in this comprehensive course for beginners with three technical tutorials. You will learn what DevOps is, continuous integration, continuous deployment strategies, and application performance management. Many DevOps practices are commonly used in programming and web development, it is important to understand key terms and technologies. 

This course often references the MERN (MongoDB, Express JS, React JS, Node JS) technology stack. You will get a series of talks with technology recommendations based on these best practices. There will be several programming examples embedded in these talks. As long as you know the absolute basics of coding and the web, you'll have no problem following along. 

⭐️ Course Notes ⭐️
🔗 Lesson 1: https://layerci.com/blog/what-is-devops/

videoTitle: ChatGPT Tutorial - Use ChatGPT for DevOps tasks to 10x Your Productivity
videoDescription:
ChatGPT Tutorial for DevOps - What is ChatGPT? How to use ChatGPT? My opinion on whether ChatGPT will replace engineers
💛  Follow me on LinkedIn and IG for weekly updates   ►  https://bit.ly/2F3LXYJ
💙  Become a DevOps Engineer - 6-month program       ►  https://bit.ly/43nK7ra
💚  Mini-Bootcamp for IT Beginners                                  ►  https://bit.ly/3BRz6To

#chatgpt   #techworldwithnana 

►  This video is sponsored by Firefly 🙌🏼
►  For more infos: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-Lf0
►  Open source tool AIaC: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-GD0

I'm sure you have all heard of ChatGPT by now. It has become a buzzword within days of its release and professionals in all fields, especially in high skilled areas like lawyers, doctors, engineers are questioning whether such AI can actually replace them and work.

So in this video I want to talk about what ChatGPT is and how it even popped up, talk a bit about the organization behind GPT called "OpenAI", which has already created many other machine learning models besides Chat GPT and also explain technically about all that.
And then we'll dive in and actually put ChatGPT to use for some DevOps related tasks.
I really want to see how it can help in generating configuration code for building DevOps processes or different parts of those processes and how well it knows different DevOps technologies, but not just some shallow examples or boilerplate code that I can get from official documentation, but instead also try more fine-tuning and small optimizations in that configuration code.

We're also going to check out an open source command line tool that is built on top of ChatGPT and was specifically created for engineers to generate infrastructure as code templates and more and finally we'll talk about the impact of ChatGPT, the quality and usefulness of such a tool for engineers and whether it will really replace the engineers and to what extent you should be concerned.

53:53 - Tools built on top of OpenAI's API

01:01:00 - My opinion on ChatGPT & whether ChatGPT will replace engineers

#edureka #DevOpsEdureka #DevOps #DevOpsTools #DevOpsTraining #DevOpsTutorial #DevOpsJenkins

About the Course
Edureka’s DevOps online training is designed to help you master key tools of Devops lifecycle like Docker, Puppet, Jenkins, Nagios, GIT, Ansible, SaltStack and Chef used by a DevOps Engineer for automating multiple steps in SDLC. During this course, our expert DevOps instructors will help you:

1. Understand the concepts and necessities of DevOps
2. Understand the need for DevOps and the day-to-day real-life problems it resolves
3. Learn installation and configuration of common infrastructure servers like Apache, and Nginx for the Enterprise
4. Learn popular DevOps tools like Jenkins, Puppet, Chef, Ansible, SaltStack, Nagios and GIT
5. Implement automated system update, installations and deployments 
6. Learn Virtualization Concepts 
7. Configuration deployment and packaging, continuous integration using GIT
8. Fine tune Performance and set-up basic Security for Infrastructure 
9. Manage server operations using Code which is popularly known as Infrastructure as a Code
10. Understand the need for and concepts of Monitoring and Logging. 

Along with the above mentioned topics, to help you master the most popular DevOps tools, you will also receive 3 additional self-paced courses including presentations, class recordings, assignments, solutions for the following tools:

1: Ansible - Covers Introduction, Setup & Configuration, Ansible Playbooks, 37 Ansible Modules, Different Roles and Command Line usage. 2: Chef - Covers Introduction, Building the Cook Book, Node Object & Search, Data-bags, Chef environment, Roles, Deploying Nodes in Production and using the Open Source Chef Server.

3: Puppet - Covers Puppet Infrastructure & run-cycle, the Puppet Language, Environment defining Nodes and Modules, Provisioning a Web Server and Executing Modules Against A Puppet Master.

Who should go for this course?
DevOps practitioners are among the highest paid IT professionals today, and the market demand for them is growing rapidly. With emergence of new job roles around DevOps philosophy, anyone aspiring to get into these new roles, can take up this DevOps course. Some of these roles are:

1. DevOps Architect
2. Automation Engineer
3. Software Tester
4. Security Engineer
5. Integration Specialist
6. Release Manager

Project Work
1. Host a dummy webpage using Apache Web Server.
2. Write shell script which reports:
a) Various system configurations related to the user and the OS.
b) Data related to load on the server.
c) Top 5 processes with maximum number of threads.
d) Sort the services by memory
3. Install Nagios on a VM node for monitoring the various parameter of the VM.

videoDescription:

videoTitle: The ONLY Roadmap to Get a DevOps Job in 2026 | Zero to Hero Guidance | Senior DevOps Engineer | USA
videoDescription:
This is the exact roadmap that will get you hired in 2026. 🚀

Stop following random roadmaps that leave you more confused than when you started. The DevOps landscape is shifting, and simply knowing a few tools isn't enough anymore. To land a high-paying role today, you need to understand the logic, the architecture, and the "why" behind every deployment.

I’m Avi (Vamsi Avinash Gunji). I’ve spent years navigating the enterprise tech world—from my first role at Clarivate in 2019 to roles at Farmers Insurance, The Home Depot, PwC, and Goldman Sachs, leading up to my current position as a Senior DevOps Engineer. I’ve seen what actually works in production and what’s just "tutorial noise."

In this video, I’m breaking down the definitive path I would follow if I were starting from zero today. We’re debugging your career "Desi style"—from code errors to chai breaks. This is my narrative—unfiltered, evolving, and unfinished.

📍 The Roadmap Breakdown:
In this video, we cover the critical phases to master:
✅ Phase 1: Mastering the Linux terminal, systemctl, and Bash/Python automation.
✅ Phase 1.2: Why Networking (DNS/VPCs) and Storage (NFS) are your best friends.
✅ Phase 2: Cloud architecture (AWS/Azure/GCP) vs. the reality of Private Cloud and On-Prem.
✅ Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform & Ansible) to save you from disasters.
✅ Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes (K8s)—The core of modern engineering.
✅ Phase 4.2: Building actual end-to-end CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins and GitHub Actions.
✅ Phase 5: Monitoring, Alerting, and Observability (Prometheus, Grafana, Splunk).

🕒 TIMESTAMPS:

00:00 Intro
00:41 Phase 1: Linux Foundations
01:40 Phase 1.2: Networking and Storage
02:48 Phase 2: The Cloud Ecosystem
04:02 Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code
04:57 Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes
06:18 Phase 4.2: CI/CD Pipelines
06:47 Phase 5: Monitoring & Alerting
07:28 Conclusion: Senior Tip

videoTitle: Cloud Engineer vs DevOps Engineer - Differences and Overlaps of tasks and responsibilities
videoDescription:
Differences and Overlaps of Cloud and DevOps Engineer Roles as well as deep dive of Cloud Engineer Tasks and Responsibilities

DevOps engineer and Cloud engineer are 2 popular roles in software engineering. But there is a lot of mix up of these two roles and many people and many companies often use them interchangeably. They mix the tasks and responsibilities of these two very often. For example there are plenty of job descriptions for "DevOps Cloud Engineer" or "Cloud DevOps Engineer", which would naturally make you think that they are the same role, but then why would you have two names for the same role? And that's exactly what I'm going to discuss and clarify in this video.

videoTitle: What is Platform Engineering and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
videoDescription:
Platform Engineering - What it is, how it works and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
Also how to approach implementing an IDP - internal developer platform.

🔗💚   Become a DevOps Engineer in 6 months       ►   https://bit.ly/3WKUPGf
🔗💙   Become a Kubernetes Administrator              ►   https://bit.ly/3IMJUWI
🔗🧡   Complete GitLab CI/CD course                        ►   https://bit.ly/42itb4B
🔗 💜  DevSecOps Bootcamp                                       ►   https://bit.ly/3OI6y66

▬▬▬▬▬▬ Thank you Pulumi for sponsoring this video 👏  ▬▬▬▬▬▬
►   Learn more about Pulumi's Internal developer platform-in-a-box: https://pulumip.us/IDP-Platform-Engineering
►   Join PulumiUp - a global Infrastructure as Code conference: https://www.pulumi.com/pulumi-up/

In this video we're going to talk about a relatively new hot topic in DevOps and Cloud space, which is “platform engineering”. There is a lot of discussion going on, where some people are asking whether platform engineering replaces DevOps. Many people say it goes hand in hand and is rather an addition to DevOps, but in reality it's a bit more complicated than that. Platform engineering actually changes a lot of established rules we knew about DevOps, SRE and Cloud engineering, it changes the game and introduces some new rules.

So let's clearly define, what platform engineering exactly is, more interestingly, why was there even a need for this new role and how it evolved and of course how it compares to DevOps and Cloud engineering and does it really replace any of these roles?

videoTitle: DevOps Interview Questions & Answers! (How to PASS a DevOps Engineer Interview!)
videoDescription:
DevOps Interview Questions & Answers by Richard McMunn of: https://passmyinterview.com/devops-engineer-interview/
#devops #devopsengineer #devopsinterviewquestions
In this interview tips training tutorial, Richard McMunn will cover:

1. A list of DEVOPS interview questions I recommend you prepare for;
2. Example ANSWERS to help you PASS your DevOps Engineer interview;
3. The QUALITIES you MUST demonstrate during your DevOps interview;
4. Further resources to help you PASS your DevOps Engineer interview!

HERE'S A FULL LIST OF DEVOPS ENGINEER INTERVIEW QUESTIONS
Q1. Tell me about yourself and why you want to work in this DevOps role?
Q2. What are the most important skills and qualities needed to work as a DevOps Engineer?
Q3. Why do you want to work for our organization in this DevOps role?
Q4. Tell me about the hardest day you’ve had as a DevOps Engineer?
Q5. What are your strengths and weaknesses?
Q6. You are giving a presentation to a number of non-technical company managers. They have no technical knowledge and they are struggling to understand your presentation. How would you adapt your presentation, so they all understand?
Q7. Where do you see yourself in the future?
Q8. How do you handle stress and pressure at work?
Q9. What does DevOps mean to you and what is its purpose in our organization?
Q10. Tell me how DevOps differs from Agile / SDLC?
Q11. What is an inode on Linux?
Q12. In your opinion, which are the top DevOps tools and what are they used for?
Q13. Tell me how you'd go about optimizing a database?
Q14. What is garbage collection programming?
Q15. Talk me through the steps you would follow when checking physical memory on Linux?
Q16. Explain how you copy a local file to HDFS?
Q17. Describe how you would make a background process run?
Q18. Assuming there are no cookies, how would you make your application work?
Q19. Talk me through the process of what happens when someone accesses a URL via a web browser?
Q20. What is the difference between CI and CD?
Q21. Tell me how you would measure network packet?
Q22. What is a distributed cache in Hadoop?
Q23. There are a large number of requests due to high demand. How would you ensure your database works?
Q24. Why do you want to leave your current job?
Q25. What are your salary expectations in this DevOps Engineer role?
Q26. That’s the end of your DevOps interview, do you have any questions for the panel?

Connect with Richard McMunn on LinkedIn.com:
https://www.linkedin.com/in/richard-mcmunn-coach/

Download Richard's 26 DevOps Engineer Interview Questions and Answers guide in PDF:
https://passmyinterview.com/devops-engineer-interview/

channelDescription: CareerVidz was founded by interview expert Richard McMunn in 2011 and it specialises in teaching job-seekers everything there is to know about careers, tests and interview questions and answers. Thanks to YouTube, Richard McMunn has put together two decades of his own expertise in careers and education whilst working in the emergency services and the armed forces. He has compiled all of this information into easy to view, step-by-step video tutorials that will help you pass your job interview or test. At the CareerVidz channel, you will find Richard McMunn often talking about his own experiences and how they have helped thousands of job-seekers and students to 'get the advantage' over other people by using key tips and strategies guaranteed to bring success. Why AWS is important?

There are various upcoming companies who have leveraged AWS cloud solutions for their growth. AWS was the first company which started to provide cloud solutions on a large scale. DevOps implementation is going through the roof with most of the largest software organizations around the world invested heavily in its implementation. videoTitle: D365 F&O: Full Dev Environment Setup (LCS, DevOps, VM) | Part 1
videoDescription:
Set up a complete D365 F&O project from scratch. Learn to configure Lifecycle Services (LCS), Azure DevOps, and a local development VM.

───────────
🕒 Timestamps
───────────

0:00 → Intro & Project Overview
1:03 → Business Scenario: Bluestone Industrial Components (BIC)
2:05 → Video Goal: The Development Foundation
2:25 → Part 1: Lifecycle Services (LCS) Setup
3:01 → Creating a new LCS Implementation Project
5:19 → Understanding the LCS Project Dashboard
7:03 → Part 2: Azure DevOps (ADO) Setup
7:30 → Creating a new ADO Organization
8:22 → Configuring Organization Settings for LCS (TFVC & Classic Pipelines)
10:26 → Creating a new ADO Project (TFVC)
11:37 → Understanding the ADO Project Interface
13:16 → Part 3: Connecting LCS and Azure DevOps
13:34 → Starting the LCS Project Onboarding Wizard
16:39 → Defining the Project Team
17:58 → Defining Project Milestones
19:00 → Generating an ADO Personal Access Token (PAT)
20:34 → Linking LCS to the ADO Project
22:10 → Part 4: Configuring the Azure Subscription
22:54 → Granting "Contributor" role to Dynamics Deployment Services
25:04 → Creating the Azure Connector in LCS
27:41 → Part 5: Deploying a Cloud Sandbox Environment
28:17 → Configuring Deployment Settings
30:40 → Monitoring Deployment & Logging In
31:42 → Part 6: Setting Up a Local Development VM
31:42 → Downloading the VHD from Shared Asset Library
32:45 → Extracting the VHD files
33:56 → Enabling Hyper-V in Windows Features
34:40 → Creating a new Virtual Machine in Hyper-V
36:17 → Attaching the VHD file
37:04 → Starting and Connecting to the VM
37:27 → Part 7: Configuring VM Authentication
37:58 → Creating an Azure App Registration
38:48 → Running the Self-Signed Certificate PowerShell Script
39:30 → Running the Admin User Provisioning Tool
40:14 → Accessing F&O Locally via IIS
41:29 → Recap

──────────────
💡 What You'll Learn
──────────────

✓ How to provision a new Lifecycle Services (LCS) implementation project.
✓ Configure Azure DevOps for D365 (including TFVC & Classic Pipeline settings).
✓ The critical step of connecting LCS to Azure DevOps for integration.
✓ How to deploy and access a local development VHD using Hyper-V.
✓ Authenticate the local VM using Azure App Registration.

──────────────────────────
🚀 About This D365 Development Series
──────────────────────────

This series goes beyond basic tutorials to show you how real-world Dynamics 365 (D365FO) projects are built from start to finish. We'll build a complete, end-to-end "Automated Customer Tiering System" for a fictional client, Bluestone Industrial Components (BIC).

You'll see every step of a production-ready solution, from setup to deployment, covering:
➤ Core X++ Development: Extending tables and forms (CustTable), creating Enums, and implementing custom business logic using Chain of Command (CoC).
➤ Automation: Building robust, automated batch jobs with the SysOperation Framework.
➤ APIs & Integration: Creating Data Entities to build secure OData REST APIs and for Data Management Framework (DMF) exports.
➤ Event-Driven Architecture: Firing real-time alerts from D365 to Azure Logic Apps using Business Events.
➤ Business Intelligence: Connecting D365 to Power BI to build an interactive sales dashboard.
➤ DevOps & ALM: Setting up a complete CI/CD pipeline in Azure DevOps, managing source control (TFVC), and automating builds.

────────────────
💻 Tools & Technologies
────────────────

🔹Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365FO)
🔹X++
🔹Visual Studio 2019
🔹Lifecycle Services (LCS)
🔹Azure DevOps (ADO)
🔹SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
🔹Power BI Desktop
🔹Azure Logic Apps

───────────────────
💬 Feedback & Future Topics
───────────────────

This series covers the core components of a practical, end-to-end D365 project. I know there are many more topics we could dive into, from advanced debugging to complex form patterns.

I welcome your feedback and ideas! If there's a specific D365 topic you'd like to see covered in a future video, please share your suggestions in the comments section below.

#Dynamics365, #D365Development, #LifecycleServices, #AzureDevOps

videoTitle: DevOps in 2026: Stay in DevOps or Transition to MLOps, AIOps, DataOps?
videoDescription:
As we close out 2025, many DevOps engineers are facing the same question:
Should I continue growing in DevOps, or is it time to transition into MLOps, AIOps, or DataOps?

With AI agents and agentic workflows increasingly automating infrastructure and operations, it’s natural to wonder how these changes impact DevOps roles and career paths going into 2026.

In this video, I break down what’s actually changing — and what isn’t. You’ll get a clear, practical explanation of DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, and DataOps, how AI agents fit across all of these domains, and how to think about your career without chasing hype or fear-driven narratives.

What you’ll learn in this video:

Why DevOps is not dying, but evolving into specialized domains

The real difference between DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, and DataOps

Where AI agents and agentic workflows actually fit in operations

When it makes sense to stay in DevOps

When transitioning to MLOps, AIOps, or DataOps is the right move

Why these paths are not mutually exclusive

How to choose a direction based on the problems that genuinely excite you

This video is designed for engineers who want clarity, not clickbait — and who want to make career decisions grounded in real systems, real demand, and real interest.

If this helped you understand where the industry is heading, consider subscribing. In upcoming videos, I’ll go deeper into each specialization — DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, and DataOps — with real-world context and practical insights.

#DevOps #MLOps #AIOps #DataOps #DevOpsCareer #AIAgents #AgenticWorkflows #CloudEngineering #FutureOfDevOps

videoTitle: D365 F&O: Setup an Azure DevOps CI Build Pipeline | Part 7
videoDescription:
Set up a CI pipeline for D365 in Azure DevOps. Learn to deploy a Build VM and automate your code builds for team development.

───────────
🕒 Timestamps
───────────

0:00 → Intro: Why We Need a CI Pipeline (Continuous Integration)
0:25 → Part 1: Create a Self-Hosted Agent Pool in ADO
1:19 → Part 2: Provision the Build VM in LCS
1:54 → Selecting the "Build and Test" Topology
2:24 → Advanced Settings: Linking the ADO Agent Pool & Branch
3:04 → Selecting the VM Size (Memory Optimized)
3:47 → Part 3: Configure the Build VM (Reset Password & Connect)
4:24 → Exploring the Build VM File Structure (SDK Folder)
4:53 → Part 4: Review the Auto-Generated Build Pipeline
5:43 → Enabling Continuous Integration Triggers & Path Filters
6:23 → Part 5: Branching Strategy (Rename 'dev' to 'Main')
6:52 → Connecting Visual Studio to Source Control (Team Explorer)
7:19 → Mapping a Local Workspace
7:55 → Adding the Local Project to Source Control
8:34 → Adding the axmodulesbuild File & Checking In
9:12 → Part 6: Creating a New 'dev' Branch from 'Main'
10:06 → Part 7: How the CI Build Process Works
10:23 → Reviewing the Build Stages (Build, Sync, Package)
10:59 → Under the Hood: Pipeline Tasks vs. VM Scripts
11:53 → Part 8: Reviewing the Final Build Artifact
12:27 → Recap

──────────────
💡 What You'll Learn
──────────────

✓ Create a self-hosted Agent Pool in Azure DevOps for D365 builds.
✓ How to deploy a Build VM from LCS and connect it to your pipeline.
✓ Configure an auto-generated build pipeline with CI triggers and path filters.
✓ Connect Visual Studio to Azure DevOps (TFVC) using Team Explorer.
✓ Check in your code to automatically trigger the build and create a Deployable Package.

──────────────────────────
🚀 About This D365 Development Series
──────────────────────────

This series goes beyond basic tutorials to show you how real-world Dynamics 365 (D365FO) projects are built from start to finish. We'll build a complete, end-to-end "Automated Customer Tiering System" for a fictional client, Bluestone Industrial Components (BIC).

You'll see every step of a production-ready solution, from setup to deployment, covering:
➤ Core X++ Development: Extending tables and forms (CustTable), creating Enums, and implementing custom business logic using Chain of Command (CoC).
➤ Automation: Building robust, automated batch jobs with the SysOperation Framework.
➤ APIs & Integration: Creating Data Entities to build secure OData REST APIs and for Data Management Framework (DMF) exports.
➤ Event-Driven Architecture: Firing real-time alerts from D365 to Azure Logic Apps using Business Events.
➤ Business Intelligence: Connecting D365 to Power BI to build an interactive sales dashboard.
➤ DevOps & ALM: Setting up a complete CI/CD pipeline in Azure DevOps, managing source control (TFVC), and automating builds.

────────────────
💻 Tools & Technologies
────────────────

🔹Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365FO)
🔹X++
🔹Visual Studio 2019
🔹Lifecycle Services (LCS)
🔹Azure DevOps (ADO)
🔹SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
🔹Power BI Desktop
🔹Azure Logic Apps

───────────────────
💬 Feedback & Future Topics
───────────────────

This series covers the core components of a practical, end-to-end D365 project. I know there are many more topics we could dive into, from advanced debugging to complex form patterns.

I welcome your feedback and ideas! If there's a specific D365 topic you'd like to see covered in a future video, please share your suggestions in the comments section below.

#Dynamics365, #AzureDevOps, #ContinuousIntegration, #D365Development

videoTitle: The DevOps Strategy Behind Stable Product Launches
videoDescription:
What if your biggest product launch becomes your biggest outage?
 
In this video, Mehul Brahmbhatt, CEO & Founder of Matrix Bricks, explains how DevOps helps businesses prevent crashes, downtime, deployment failures, and launch-day chaos.
 
From infrastructure scaling to real-time monitoring, DevOps has become one of the most important foundations for successful digital product launches.


🎯 Key Takeaways

▫️Why products fail during launch despite successful testing
▫️How DevOps prepares systems for real-world traffic pressure
▫️The role of CI/CD in reducing deployment risks
▫️Why continuous testing improves launch stability
▫️How real-time monitoring prevents major downtime
▫️Why security must be integrated before launch day
▫️How collaboration between teams improves execution


👀 Why You Should Watch This Video

▫️Learn how to prevent launch-day crashes and outages
▫️Understand how high-performing tech teams prepare for scale
▫️Discover how DevOps improves performance and stability
▫️Learn how CI/CD pipelines reduce deployment errors
▫️Understand the importance of monitoring and recovery systems

videoTitle: How to Fix High CPU Usage in Production | DevOps Real-Time Scenario (Day 3)
videoDescription:
💥 Day 3 of the DevOps in ARIA Series – Real-Time Scenarios!

🎯 Today’s Interview Question:
“Your production server is showing high CPU usage — what will you do?”

This is more than an interview question — it’s a real-world DevOps challenge you’ll face on the job.

🎥 In this video, I break down:

✅ How to investigate CPU issues using tools like top, htop, Grafana, and Prometheus

✅ How to identify which process or service is consuming resources

✅ When to restart a service — and what to check before and after

✅ How code bugs or deployment issues can spike CPU

✅ When to scale infra or adjust auto-scaling policies

✅ Why documenting and learning from incidents makes you a stronger engineer

💡 Clarity, calmness, and a systematic approach — that’s the DevOps way!

📌 If you're preparing for DevOps interviews or working in production environments, this video will sharpen your troubleshooting skills.

🔗 Watch now, comment your thoughts, and don’t forget to subscribe for Day 4 and beyond!

videoTitle: 📈🎓Mastering the DevOps Engineer Road Map🌟🔧 | #thillaithetechie
videoDescription:
✅ Foundational Knowledge: Understand Linux, scripting (Python/Bash), and networking basics.

✅ Version Control: Master Git for source code management and collaboration.

✅ CI/CD Pipelines: Learn Jenkins, Travis CI, or CircleCI to automate testing and deployments.

✅ Configuration Management: Gain expertise in Ansible, Puppet, or Chef for infrastructure as code.

✅ Containers and Orchestration: Dive into Docker and Kubernetes for efficient application deployment.

✅ Cloud Platforms: Get hands-on experience with AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud for scalable solutions.

✅ Monitoring and Logging: Implement tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK stack for system monitoring.

videoTitle: Day 27 – ARM Templates & Bicep with Azure DevOps | 30 Days Azure DevOps Challenge
videoDescription:
🚀 Welcome to Day 27 of my 30 Days Azure DevOps Challenge!

🔹 Topic: ARM Templates & Bicep with Azure DevOps

Yesterday, we learned how Terraform brings Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to multiple clouds.
Today, we’re focusing on Azure-native IaC tools:

👉 Key differences:

ARM Templates → JSON files that define Azure resources.

Bicep → A cleaner, simpler language to write the same infrastructure.

✅ Why use them?

Strong native Azure support

Seamless integration with Azure DevOps

Best fit for teams working mainly within Azure

✨ Key takeaway:

Terraform = multi-cloud flexibility 🌎

ARM/Bicep = deep Azure integration & simplicity 💡

📌 Next in Day 28, I’ll cover Monitoring & Logging in Azure DevOps — making sure your apps and pipelines run reliably!

👉 Don’t forget to like 👍, share 🔁, and subscribe 🔔 for more daily DevOps learning!

videoTitle: Understand DevOps in Under 8 Minutes | #devops | Matrix Bricks
videoDescription:
DevOps is one of the most talked-about practices in tech today, but many still aren’t clear on what it actually means. In this video, Mehul Brahmbhatt, Founder and CEO of Matrix Bricks, explains DevOps in simple, practical terms for anyone new to the concept.
Whether you're a developer, product manager, or just curious about how modern software teams work, this video covers the fundamentals in under 8 minutes.

💡 Why This Video Is a Must-Watch:
⦿ Clearly explains what DevOps is and why it exists
⦿ Helps you understand how development and operations work better together
⦿ Breaks down the major challenges DevOps solves in software delivery
⦿ Covers essential principles like automation, CI/CD, and continuous feedback
⦿ Offers insight into how real teams use DevOps to improve speed and quality
 
🛠️ What You’ll Walk Away With:
⦿ A beginner-friendly understanding of DevOps without technical jargon
⦿ A grasp of how CI/CD pipelines help teams deploy faster and more reliably
⦿ Knowledge of the four key pillars that drive successful DevOps practices
⦿ A clearer view of how DevOps improves collaboration across teams
⦿ The confidence to start learning or applying DevOps in your own work

This is a quick overview designed to give you a clear understanding of the topic—no prior experience required.
📌 If you're building digital products or working in tech, understanding DevOps is essential to keeping up with how software is built and delivered today.

videoTitle: No coding to DevOps Career: Market Trends, Salary Insights & DevOps Job Opportunities in Germany.
videoDescription:
Are you considering a career in DevOps or exploring new opportunities in the field? In this video, we dive deep into the current DevOps job market, explore salary expectations, and discuss how learning German can significantly boost your chances of landing a job in Germany.

What You’ll Learn:

Free and Paid Learning Resources: Discover where you can start learning DevOps, from free YouTube tutorials to paid courses.
Job Market Trends: Understand the current job market for DevOps professionals in India and Germany.
Salary Expectations: Get insights on expected salaries for fresh graduates and experienced professionals, including potential earnings in startups, medium-sized companies, and large corporations.
Application Tips: Learn how to prepare a compelling resume, the importance of cover letters, and strategies for applying to job portals and company career sites.
Motivation Matters: Find out why showing genuine interest and motivation can make a difference in your job application.
Key Takeaways:

DevOps offers numerous career opportunities, even without a formal background in the field.
Learning German can give you a significant edge in the German job market.
Salary expectations vary based on experience and company size, with high earning potential in large firms and consulting companies.
Subscribe for more insights into career development, industry trends, and tips for success. Share your questions and thoughts in the comments below!

videoTitle: How to Sync Jira with Azure DevOps
videoDescription:
📌✨🎥 Streamline your software development workflows with Unito's 2-way Azure DevOps - Jira Integration! 🚀

Are you a project manager looking to enhance collaboration between Azure DevOps and Jira for your dev teams? Look no further! 🤝 With Unito's 2-way sync, you can seamlessly connect work items in Azure DevOps with issues in Jira, ensuring consistent and up-to-date information in real-time! ⏰

✨ Book a live demo with our team
unito.io/demo/?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=video&utm_campaign=jira_ado_demo

Unito supports essential fields in Azure DevOps and Jira, including:

💼 Title/Summary: The title or summary of the issue or work item provides a brief description of what the task or issue entails.

📝 Description: The detailed description of the issue or work item, including any additional information or context needed for team members to understand the task.

👤 Assignee: The person responsible for completing the task or addressing the issue. Keeping the assignee field in sync ensures that the correct team member is aware of their responsibilities.

🔢 Status: The current status of the issue or work item, such as "To Do," "In Progress," or "Done." Syncing the status field helps everyone stay updated on the progress of tasks.

🔝 Priority: The priority level of the issue or work item, which helps determine its importance and urgency. Syncing the priority field ensures that team members are aligned on task prioritization.

🏷️ Labels/Tags: Categorizing issues or work items with labels or tags helps with organization and filtering. Keeping these fields in sync ensures consistent labeling across both platforms.

💬 Comments/Updates: Any comments or updates made on an issue or work item should be synced to provide real-time collaboration and visibility for everyone involved.

⏰ Due Date: If there is a specific deadline or due date associated with the task or issue, syncing this field helps the team stay on track and meet deadlines.

📎 Attachments: Any files or attachments related to the issue or work item should be synced to ensure easy access and collaboration.

📌 Work Item Type/Issue Type: The type of work item or issue, such as bug, feature, or task. Syncing this field provides clarity and consistency in categorizing different types of work.

Try Unito's Azure DevOps - Jira integration for free today and eliminate manual updates and double-entry of data! ⚡️✨ Create a Jira issue and watch as it automatically generates a corresponding Azure DevOps task using Unito's powerful 2-way sync. Any changes made in either system will be automatically updated in the synchronized platform, keeping everyone on the same page. 💪

Customize your sync by selecting specific fields and custom fields, tailoring it to your software development team's unique needs. Unito empowers project managers to achieve greater efficiency and accuracy in their workflows, leading to enhanced collaboration and better results. 💡

videoTitle: DevOps: SRE, SLA, SLO, SLI Site Reliability Engineering, Service Level Agreement Objective Indicator
videoDescription:
Heard about SRE (Site Reliability Engineering), SLA (Service Level Agreement), SLO (Service Level Objective), SLI (Service Level Indicator), but unclear about it or want to know more about it? Need a simple explanation or some examples? Then this might be the right video for you.

Talking about a short history of SRE, its origin, its relation with DevOps / DevSecOps, about differences between SLA, SLO, and SLI how they relate one to each other.

At the end we will see some simple concrete examples that are easy to understand.

If you like the content or found the content useful, please don't forget to like the video and subscribe. Its free but helps me to create more contents.

videoTitle: C'est quoi le DevOps ? Tout comprendre en deux minutes ! CI/CD, feedback et qualité !

channelDescription:
GitHub is the AI-powered developer platform to build, scale, and deliver secure software. We make it easier for developers to be developers: to work together, to solve challenging problems, and to create the world’s most important technologies. We foster a collaborative community that can come together—as individuals and in teams—to create the future of software and make a difference in the world.

The GitHub platform is where developers can create, share, and ship the best code possible. Our community is made up of students, hobbyists, consultants, enterprise professionals, partners, and executives, building software in the way that works best for them.

GitHub is much more than code. It is the home of open-source collaboration. It is where new developers get started and where experienced developers expand their knowledge. It is a community where developers come together to solve the unsolvable and test the limits of what software can do.

About AWS:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) hosts events, both online and in-person, bringing the cloud computing community together to connect, collaborate, and learn from AWS experts. AWS is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform, offering over 200 fully featured services from data centers globally. Millions of customers—including the fastest-growing startups, largest enterprises, and leading government agencies—are using AWS to lower costs, become more agile, and innovate faster.

#AWSreInvent  #AWSreInvent2024
channelDescription:
The official YouTube channel for AWS Events and Webinars. Amazon Web Services (AWS) hosts events, both online and in-person, bringing the cloud computing community together to connect, collaborate, and learn from AWS experts.

AWS re:Invent is a learning conference for the global cloud computing community. The event features keynote announcements, training and certification opportunities, access to technical sessions, deep-dives with AWS leaders, engaging with sponsors, and so much more.

AWS re:Inforce is a two day conference focused on cloud security. Attendees can expect two days of learning, with sessions on security, identity, and compliance. 

AWS Summits is a series of events that bring the cloud computing community together to connect, collaborate, and learn about AWS. 

AWSome Day Online Conference is a free, online training event that will provide a step-by-step introduction to the core AWS services for compute, storage, databases, and networking.

videoTitle: Understand DevOps in Under 8 Minutes | #devops | Matrix Bricks
videoDescription:

Whether you're a developer, product manager, or just curious about how modern software teams work, this video covers the fundamentals in under 8 minutes.

⦿ Offers insight into how real teams use DevOps to improve speed and quality
 
🛠️ What You’ll Walk Away With:
⦿ A beginner-friendly understanding of DevOps without technical jargon
⦿ A grasp of how CI/CD pipelines help teams deploy faster and more reliably
⦿ Knowledge of the four key pillars that drive successful DevOps practices

This is a quick overview designed to give you a clear understanding of the topic—no prior experience required.

videoTitle: Guided DevOps Meditation
videoDescription:
Dive into a unique meditative experience designed specifically for the DevOps Engineer. In the hustle of ensuring seamless deployments and system operations, every DevOps professional deserves a moment to reboot, refresh, and recenter.

Whether you're in the midst of a complex project or wrapping up a day of successful deployments, this meditation will guide you to find serenity amidst the codes and systems.

🔗 Find tranquility amidst the binaries. Hit play and embark on a journey to the calm side of DevOps.

Don't forget to LIKE, SHARE, and SUBSCRIBE for more content dedicated to the DevOps community! 🚀👍

▬▬▬▬▬▬ S C R I P T  📜  ▬▬▬▬▬▬

"Begin by finding a serene place where you can sit undisturbed. As you prepare to delve into a state of calm, think of it as setting up the perfect environment for a seamless deployment."

"Sit or lie down in a comfortable position, ensuring that your back is straight and your hands rest lightly on your lap or by your sides. Close your eyes gently. Let's begin our system reboot."

"Start by taking a deep breath in, inhaling calmness and clarity, like the smooth initiation of a well-planned product. And exhale, releasing any system glitches or unwanted bugs from your mind."

"Imagine your mind as a server room. Each server represents a thought or a responsibility in your life. Some servers are humming busily, while others are quiet and in standby mode."

"Visualize a cooling system, a gust of fresh, clean air entering the room, stabilizing the temperature. This cooling breath ensures that every server, every thought, operates at its peak without overheating."

"As you continue breathing deeply, bring to mind a continuous integration and continuous deployment pipeline. With every inhale, new features and positive affirmations are integrated. With every exhale, new code is deployed to production."

"Imagine a monitoring dashboard, showcasing green status indicators. Your body is this system. Your heart rate, your breathing, your state of calm are all in optimal state - all systems operational and running smoothly."

"Think about the code commits. Sometimes, errors occur, and that's okay. Acknowledge them without judgment, fix them, and move on. For now, release any attachment to those errors, letting them float away like logs being archived."

"Your breathing is now your main server, your central command. Focus on its rhythm, the gentle in-and-out, the same predictable yet vital process, akin to the regular checks and balances in DevOps."

"Even in the face of challenges, remember that every system has its recovery procedure. Your mind, your spirit, and your body are no different. You have the resilience, the backup plans, and the capability for recovery."

"Slowly begin to transition, like smoothly moving from development to production. Become aware of your surroundings. Feel the chair or the floor supporting you, the gentle rhythm of your heart."

"When you're ready, with the confidence of a successful code deployment, open your eyes. As you go back into the world, remember that just like in DevOps, balance, continuous improvement, and adaptation are key."

▬▬▬▬▬▬  S U B S C R I B E  🔔 ▬▬▬▬▬▬

videoTitle: No coding to DevOps Career: Market Trends, Salary Insights & DevOps Job Opportunities in Germany.
videoDescription:
Are you considering a career in DevOps or exploring new opportunities in the field? In this video, we dive deep into the current DevOps job market, explore salary expectations, and discuss how learning German can significantly boost your chances of landing a job in Germany.

What You’ll Learn:

Free and Paid Learning Resources: Discover where you can start learning DevOps, from free YouTube tutorials to paid courses.
Job Market Trends: Understand the current job market for DevOps professionals in India and Germany.
Salary Expectations: Get insights on expected salaries for fresh graduates and experienced professionals, including potential earnings in startups, medium-sized companies, and large corporations.
Application Tips: Learn how to prepare a compelling resume, the importance of cover letters, and strategies for applying to job portals and company career sites.
Motivation Matters: Find out why showing genuine interest and motivation can make a difference in your job application.
Key Takeaways:

DevOps offers numerous career opportunities, even without a formal background in the field.
Learning German can give you a significant edge in the German job market.
Salary expectations vary based on experience and company size, with high earning potential in large firms and consulting companies.
Subscribe for more insights into career development, industry trends, and tips for success. Share your questions and thoughts in the comments below!

videoTitle: Introduction of DevOps Certified Professional Program
videoDescription:
The DevOps Certified Professional Program is a comprehensive training and certification program designed to equip professionals with the skills and knowledge required to excel in the field of DevOps. DevOps is a set of practices that combines software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) to promote collaboration, automation, and continuous delivery.

The DevOps Certified Professional Program offers a structured curriculum that covers various aspects of DevOps, including continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD), infrastructure automation, cloud computing, containerization, configuration management, monitoring, and more. The program is designed to provide a holistic understanding of the DevOps principles, tools, and best practices.

Participants in the program gain hands-on experience through practical exercises, case studies, and real-world projects. They learn to leverage popular DevOps tools and technologies such as Git, Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, and Prometheus. The program also emphasizes the cultural aspects of DevOps, focusing on collaboration, communication, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.

Upon completing the program, participants have the opportunity to earn a DevOps Certified Professional certification, which validates their proficiency and expertise in DevOps principles and practices. This certification can enhance career prospects and demonstrate a commitment to professional development in the field of DevOps.

The DevOps Certified Professional Program is suitable for professionals from various backgrounds, including software development, system administration, operations, quality assurance, and project management. It is particularly beneficial for individuals seeking to transition into DevOps roles or enhance their existing DevOps skills.

Overall, the DevOps Certified Professional Program provides a comprehensive and structured learning path for individuals looking to build a strong foundation in DevOps practices and technologies. It equips participants with the skills and certification necessary to excel in today's fast-paced and collaborative software development and operations environment.

videoTitle: 😱Hey DevOps, you’re killing my job 😢!
videoDescription:
😱Hey DevOps, you’re killing my job 😢!
Really? No 😉.
Don't panic and check out the talk 🎞 I had with Pia Wiedermayer on this topic.

Is DevOps really the reason why testing and quality assurance (QA) employees are being increasingly automated out of a job?
Pia Wiedermayer, Head of QA, and Romano Roth, Head of DevOps, discuss different ways to incorporate the wealth of experience of testing and QA specialists into the agile team culture.

Insight: 
- DevOps is often equated with automation
- What does this mean for people in these roles and with these responsibilities?
- Testers offer a wealth of experience that can be incorporated into the
agile team culture

Software testing and quality assurance are changing. Topics such as agility, DevOps and automation strongly influence existing roles and areas of responsibility in software quality assurance as well as during the entire software life cycle. Standard testing and QA roles no longer exist in many (project) organisations. Dedicated testing teams are increasingly a thing of the past.

What does this mean for people in these roles and with these responsibilities? Is there no room for them anymore? Are their skills and expertise no longer needed?

DevOps is often equated with automation. Is DevOps responsible for automating employees out of a job? What is behind the hype and who or what is DevOps actually?

Pia Wiedermayer, Head of Quality Assurance https://www.linkedin.com/in/piawiedermayer/, and Romano Roth, Head of DevOps, at Zühlke Switzerland tackle these questions. They talk about the challenges in their projects, examine roles and tasks and reveal new paths for professional and personal development. They also discuss ways to incorporate the wealth of experience of testers into the agile team culture and whether automation is the new testing.

▬▬▬▬▬▬ T I M E S T A M P S ⏰  ▬▬▬▬▬▬
00:00 Introduction
00:19 How is your project?
01:18 How was it in the past? 
01:53 How is it today? 
02:53 Does DevOps help?
03:23 What do you think about that? 
03:49 What is DevOps?
04:33 Who is DevOps? 
05:00 Your role in the past
05:26 Your role in the future
06:06 How to automate? 
08:00 1. Quality assurace and the corresponding skills are integral parts of every step in the DevOps cycle
08:13 2. Enable the team to improve quality. QA coach and share responsibility for quality and testing
08:25 3. Use tools an automation to increase efficiency 
08:38 4. Despite all tools and frameworks, it is the people who make the project successful
08:54 Finish

videoTitle: Secure  secrets with Azure KeyVault using DevOps Variable Groups
videoDescription:
Secure application secrets with Azure KeyVault using DevOps Variable Groups.


Arguments:
-DBNAME "$(DBNAME)" -DBUSERNAME "$(DBUserName)" -PASWORD "$(Password)"

Inline Script :
Param(
[string]$DNBAME,
[string]$DBUSERNAME ,
[string]$PASWORD
)

Write-Host "##vso[task.setvariable variable=DNBANE]$DBNAME"
Write-Host "##vso[task.setvariable variable=DBUSERNAME]$DBUSERNAME"
Write-Host "##vso[task.setvariable variable=PASWORD]$PASWORD"

Why devops is important?

DevOps implementation is going through the roof with most of the largest software organizations around the world invested heavily in its implementation. The core values of devops is effectively based on the Agile Manifesto but with one slight change which moves the focus from creating a working software to one that is more interested in the end-to-end software service mechanism and delivery.

Why should you opt for a DevOps career?

For very long times the development and the operations teams of any software enterprise have stayed at arm’s length. But this organizational cultural shift thanks to devops a lot of changes are happening in forward-thinking enterprises. Learning devops will help you master all the skills needed in order to successfully build, operate, monitor, measure and improve the various processes in IT enterprises by better integrating development and operations. You will grab the best jobs in top MNCs after finishing this Intellipaat devops online training. The entire Intellipaat devops course is in line with the industry needs. videoTitle: How to Sync Jira with Azure DevOps
videoDescription:
📌✨🎥 Streamline your software development workflows with Unito's 2-way Azure DevOps - Jira Integration! 🚀

Are you a project manager looking to enhance collaboration between Azure DevOps and Jira for your dev teams? Look no further! 🤝 With Unito's 2-way sync, you can seamlessly connect work items in Azure DevOps with issues in Jira, ensuring consistent and up-to-date information in real-time! ⏰

✨ Book a live demo with our team
unito.io/demo/?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=video&utm_campaign=jira_ado_demo

Unito supports essential fields in Azure DevOps and Jira, including:

💼 Title/Summary: The title or summary of the issue or work item provides a brief description of what the task or issue entails.

📝 Description: The detailed description of the issue or work item, including any additional information or context needed for team members to understand the task.

👤 Assignee: The person responsible for completing the task or addressing the issue. Keeping the assignee field in sync ensures that the correct team member is aware of their responsibilities.

🔢 Status: The current status of the issue or work item, such as "To Do," "In Progress," or "Done." Syncing the status field helps everyone stay updated on the progress of tasks.

🔝 Priority: The priority level of the issue or work item, which helps determine its importance and urgency. Syncing the priority field ensures that team members are aligned on task prioritization.

🏷️ Labels/Tags: Categorizing issues or work items with labels or tags helps with organization and filtering. Keeping these fields in sync ensures consistent labeling across both platforms.

💬 Comments/Updates: Any comments or updates made on an issue or work item should be synced to provide real-time collaboration and visibility for everyone involved.

⏰ Due Date: If there is a specific deadline or due date associated with the task or issue, syncing this field helps the team stay on track and meet deadlines.

📎 Attachments: Any files or attachments related to the issue or work item should be synced to ensure easy access and collaboration.

📌 Work Item Type/Issue Type: The type of work item or issue, such as bug, feature, or task. Syncing this field provides clarity and consistency in categorizing different types of work.

Try Unito's Azure DevOps - Jira integration for free today and eliminate manual updates and double-entry of data! ⚡️✨ Create a Jira issue and watch as it automatically generates a corresponding Azure DevOps task using Unito's powerful 2-way sync. Any changes made in either system will be automatically updated in the synchronized platform, keeping everyone on the same page. 💪

Customize your sync by selecting specific fields and custom fields, tailoring it to your software development team's unique needs. Unito empowers project managers to achieve greater efficiency and accuracy in their workflows, leading to enhanced collaboration and better results. 💡

channelDescription:
Unito's 2-way integrations connect SaaS applications and tools through a simple self-serve, point-and-click interface. So your manual changes in one app automatically update the other in real-time. That's the power of 2-way sync. 

Use it to sync tasks, tickets, issues, spreadsheet rows, calendar events, contacts, and more for smoother collaboration, greater efficiency, and productivity.

Unito supports integrations for: Trello, Asana, Jira, Google Sheets, Google Calendar, Notion, ClickUp, Airtable, GitHub, Wrike, Microsoft Excel, HubSpot, monday.com, Azure DevOps, Smartsheet, Zendesk, Teamwork, Salesforce and other popular work management tools. 

Our latest batch of connectors includes: Google Analytics 4 (GA4), Facebook Ads Manager, Google Ads, Confluence, Eloqua, ServiceNow, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, Xero, Stripe, Jotform, Typeform, and SurveyMonkey.

Find out more
unito.io

Learn to use the platform
unito.io/resources/

videoTitle: DevOps: SRE, SLA, SLO, SLI Site Reliability Engineering, Service Level Agreement Objective Indicator
videoDescription:
Heard about SRE (Site Reliability Engineering), SLA (Service Level Agreement), SLO (Service Level Objective), SLI (Service Level Indicator), but unclear about it or want to know more about it? Need a simple explanation or some examples? Then this might be the right video for you.

Talking about a short history of SRE, its origin, its relation with DevOps / DevSecOps, about differences between SLA, SLO, and SLI how they relate one to each other.

At the end we will see some simple concrete examples that are easy to understand.

If you like the content or found the content useful, please don't forget to like the video and subscribe. Its free but helps me to create more contents.

Many thanks, feel free to share feedback and ideas. Your feedback and comments are appreciated.

Chapters / Sections
00:00 SRE, SLA, SLO, SLI Video Contents Overview
00:27 SRE / Site Reliability Engineering History
01:33 SRE vs DevOps
02:03 SLA vs SLO vs SLI Definition
02:40 SLA, SLO, SLI Relationship
03:41 SLA, SLO, SLI Examples

videoTitle: GitHub vs GitLab: Which DevOps Platform is Best for Your Project?
videoDescription:
The ultimate fight for the modern developer workflow comes down to two giants: GitHub and GitLab. They both start with Git, but their fundamental philosophies on how you build, test, and deploy software couldn't be more different. Are you better off with GitHub's flexible "Best-of-Breed" Marketplace approach, or is GitLab's unified, "All-in-One" DevSecOps platform the right choice for your enterprise?

In this comprehensive comparison, I break down the core features, CI/CD platforms (GitHub Actions vs. GitLab CI/CD), security models, and hosting flexibility to help you make the definitive decision for your team, whether you're working on open-source, a complex enterprise pipeline, or a heavily regulated project.

Watch the full video to find out which platform is the absolute best fit for your specific needs!

What do you think? Which platform are you using for your current project and why? Let me know in the comments below!

If you enjoy the video,  like it, and subscribe to my channel!
See you in the next video!

videoTitle: How to Create Cron Jobs in Azure DevOps Pipelines  | Automate Your Workflow
videoDescription:

By the end of this tutorial, you'll understand how to define cron jobs in your Azure DevOps pipelines, ensuring your workflows are efficient and automated. Cron jobs are essential for running scheduled tasks, such as backups, updates, and custom scripts, at specified intervals.

**Key Takeaways:**

- Understanding cron jobs and their importance in automation.
- Setting up your Azure DevOps project for pipeline creation.
- Creating a new pipeline and defining cron jobs in YAML.

videoTitle: Kubernetes Ingress vs Gateway API on AWS EKS with Hands-on #devops #aws #ingress #gateway
videoDescription:
Kubernetes Ingress vs Gateway API on AWS EKS

Github Repo : https://github.com/RohanRusta21/mern-k8s.git

Timestamp:
Understand Concept : 00:00
Nginx Ingress on EKS cluster : 21:17
Nginx Fabric Gateway on EKS cluster : 28:00

1. NGINX Ingress Controller
What is NGINX Ingress?

Ingress is a Kubernetes resource that manages external HTTP/HTTPS access to services in a cluster.
NGINX Ingress Controller is an implementation of the Kubernetes Ingress specification using NGINX as the reverse proxy and load balancer.
It dynamically configures NGINX to route traffic to the appropriate services based on rules defined in Ingress resources.


Key Features

Traffic Routing: Routes external traffic to services using hostnames, paths, or headers.
Load Balancing: Distributes traffic across pods to improve performance and reliability.
SSL/TLS Termination: Handles HTTPS traffic and certificate management.
Dynamic Configuration: Automatically updates NGINX configuration when Ingress rules change.
High Availability: Supports multiple replicas for fault tolerance.


How It Works

Ingress Resource: Define routing rules (e.g., example.com/api → api-service).
Ingress Controller: Watches for changes to Ingress resources and updates NGINX configuration.
NGINX: Acts as a reverse proxy, forwarding requests to the correct backend services.


Use Cases

Exposing multiple services under a single domain.
Managing SSL certificates for secure communication.
Load balancing and traffic management in microservices architectures.


2. NGINX Fabric Gateway
What is NGINX Fabric Gateway?

NGINX Fabric Gateway is a component of the NGINX Service Mesh (part of the NGINX Controller ecosystem).
It acts as a sidecar proxy for services in a service mesh, managing communication between services.
Unlike Ingress, which focuses on north-south traffic (external to internal), Fabric Gateway manages east-west traffic (service-to-service within the cluster).


Key Features

Service Discovery: Dynamically locates services in the mesh.
Traffic Management: Implements retries, timeouts, and circuit breaking.
Security: Enforces mutual TLS (mTLS) for secure service-to-service communication.
Observability: Provides metrics, logs, and traces for monitoring.


How It Works

Sidecar Deployment: Runs alongside each service pod.
Traffic Interception: Captures all inbound and outbound traffic for the service.
Policy Enforcement: Applies routing, security, and resilience policies.


Use Cases

Securing and managing internal service communication.
Implementing canary deployments and A/B testing.
Monitoring and troubleshooting microservices interactions.

videoTitle: D365 F&O: Setup an Azure DevOps CI Build Pipeline | Part 7
videoDescription:
Set up a CI pipeline for D365 in Azure DevOps. Learn to deploy a Build VM and automate your code builds for team development.

───────────
🕒 Timestamps
───────────

0:00 → Intro: Why We Need a CI Pipeline (Continuous Integration)
0:25 → Part 1: Create a Self-Hosted Agent Pool in ADO
1:19 → Part 2: Provision the Build VM in LCS
1:54 → Selecting the "Build and Test" Topology
2:24 → Advanced Settings: Linking the ADO Agent Pool & Branch
3:04 → Selecting the VM Size (Memory Optimized)
3:47 → Part 3: Configure the Build VM (Reset Password & Connect)
4:24 → Exploring the Build VM File Structure (SDK Folder)
4:53 → Part 4: Review the Auto-Generated Build Pipeline
5:43 → Enabling Continuous Integration Triggers & Path Filters
6:23 → Part 5: Branching Strategy (Rename 'dev' to 'Main')
6:52 → Connecting Visual Studio to Source Control (Team Explorer)
7:19 → Mapping a Local Workspace
7:55 → Adding the Local Project to Source Control
8:34 → Adding the axmodulesbuild File & Checking In
9:12 → Part 6: Creating a New 'dev' Branch from 'Main'
10:06 → Part 7: How the CI Build Process Works
10:23 → Reviewing the Build Stages (Build, Sync, Package)
10:59 → Under the Hood: Pipeline Tasks vs. VM Scripts
11:53 → Part 8: Reviewing the Final Build Artifact
12:27 → Recap

──────────────
💡 What You'll Learn
──────────────

✓ Create a self-hosted Agent Pool in Azure DevOps for D365 builds.
✓ How to deploy a Build VM from LCS and connect it to your pipeline.
✓ Configure an auto-generated build pipeline with CI triggers and path filters.
✓ Connect Visual Studio to Azure DevOps (TFVC) using Team Explorer.
✓ Check in your code to automatically trigger the build and create a Deployable Package.

──────────────────────────
🚀 About This D365 Development Series
──────────────────────────

This series goes beyond basic tutorials to show you how real-world Dynamics 365 (D365FO) projects are built from start to finish. We'll build a complete, end-to-end "Automated Customer Tiering System" for a fictional client, Bluestone Industrial Components (BIC).

You'll see every step of a production-ready solution, from setup to deployment, covering:
➤ Core X++ Development: Extending tables and forms (CustTable), creating Enums, and implementing custom business logic using Chain of Command (CoC).
➤ Automation: Building robust, automated batch jobs with the SysOperation Framework.
➤ APIs & Integration: Creating Data Entities to build secure OData REST APIs and for Data Management Framework (DMF) exports.
➤ Event-Driven Architecture: Firing real-time alerts from D365 to Azure Logic Apps using Business Events.
➤ Business Intelligence: Connecting D365 to Power BI to build an interactive sales dashboard.
➤ DevOps & ALM: Setting up a complete CI/CD pipeline in Azure DevOps, managing source control (TFVC), and automating builds.

────────────────
💻 Tools & Technologies
────────────────

🔹Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365FO)
🔹X++
🔹Visual Studio 2019
🔹Lifecycle Services (LCS)
🔹Azure DevOps (ADO)
🔹SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
🔹Power BI Desktop
🔹Azure Logic Apps

───────────────────
💬 Feedback & Future Topics
───────────────────

This series covers the core components of a practical, end-to-end D365 project. I know there are many more topics we could dive into, from advanced debugging to complex form patterns.

I welcome your feedback and ideas! If there's a specific D365 topic you'd like to see covered in a future video, please share your suggestions in the comments section below.

#Dynamics365, #AzureDevOps, #ContinuousIntegration, #D365Development

videoTitle: Is DevOps the highest paid job? | Codekido Episode 13
videoDescription:
In this episode of the Codekido series we answer the 10 most frequently asked questions about our lives as engineering professionals our career paths and the reality of the tech industry. We move beyond the code to discuss the stress the passion and the unconventional journeys that led us to DevOps.

From the origin story of the name Codekido to the debate over whether a university degree is necessary for a successful career in engineering we share our honest perspectives. We also discuss our personal backgrounds including missed opportunities in nuclear physics and medicine and how those signs led us to where we are today.

Key topics covered in this video:
The authentic story behind the name Codekido and its surprising meaning in Japanese.
Our daily routines outside of the office including gym sessions hiking and marathon study sessions for doctoral degrees.
Alternative career paths we almost took such as becoming a doctor in Germany or studying nuclear physics in Dresden.
The university vs bootcamp debate and why we believe a formal education provides a critical foundation for problem solving.
Handling the stress of the tech industry and debunking the myth that you do not need to love your job to be successful.
Why DevOps is often perceived as one of the highest paid roles and the complexity that justifies it.
The importance of believing in the process and learning from every setback on your professional journey.

Video Chapters:
00:00:00 Introduction to the most frequently asked questions
00:00:40 How the name Codekido was born and the Japanese connection
00:02:18 What we do when we are not coding fitness and hobbies
00:03:52 Alternative careers from medicine to nuclear physics
00:05:03 Do you need a university degree for a career in DevOps
00:07:05 Best and worst career advice we have ever received
00:08:21 Why passion for your work is essential for long term success
00:09:16 How to stay connected with us and send your questions

videoTitle: Create Managed Azure DevOps Pools with Terraform
videoDescription:
Today we're working with Azure Pipelines and exploring the different options you have when working with agent pools. The main focus will be on the new Azure Managed DevOps Agent Pools, what they are, how they work, and why you might want to use them.

We’ll start by deploying some prerequisites using Terraform, including a virtual network to host our agents. You’ll see how to use the AzAPI provider to get the job done and I’ll walk you through it all, step by step, as we build this Terraform project from scratch.

Once everything is up and running, we’ll connect our agent to an Azure Pipeline and run a deployment to test it out.

If you’re looking to automate Azure DevOps agent pool provisioning with Terraform and AzAPI, or just want to learn more about CI/CD in Azure, you’re in the right place. Hope you enjoy the video!

Timestamps: 
0:00 Intro
1:00 Differences between managed devops pools and other offerings

27:50 Writing Terraform for our Managed Devops pool
33:30 Deploying our code
34:10 Create pipelines and test our pool
38:20 Recap and outro

In this full step-by-step guide, you’ll learn how to set up and use Azure DevOps — from creating your first DevOps organization to deploying a Kubernetes (AKS) cluster through an automated pipeline.

Whether you’re a developer, DevOps engineer, or IT professional, this tutorial will help you master the foundations of continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) using Microsoft’s powerful DevOps platform.

What You’ll Learn in This Video:
 — What is Azure DevOps & Why Teams Use It
 — How to Create a DevOps Organization
 — How to Add a User in Azure DevOps & Assign Access Levels

videoTitle: Manage & Secure Kubernetes Cluster Policies via OPA Gatekeeper. #opa #devops #gatekeeper
videoDescription:
Manage & Secure Kubernetes Cluster Policies via OPA Gatekeeper. #opa #devops #gatekeepers 

Timestamps:

Theory/Concepts : 00:00
Hands on/Practical : 09:00

github repo : https://github.com/RohanRusta21/opa-gatekeeper.git

Concepts :

OPA Gatekeeper: Policy and Governance for Kubernetes

What is OPA:

The Open Policy Agent (OPA) is an open-source, general-purpose policy engine that unifies policy enforcement across the stack. OPA provides a high-level declarative language that lets us specify policies as code and simple APIs to offload policy decision-making from our software. We can use OPA to enforce policies in microservices, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, API gateways, and more. 
In kubernetes, OPA uses admission controllers.

What is OPA Gatekeeper?

OPA Gatekeeper is a specialized project providing first-class integration between OPA and Kubernetes.
OPA Gatekeeper adds the following on top of plain OPA:
● An extensible, parameterized policy library.
● Native Kubernetes CRDs for instantiating the policy library (aka “constraints”).
● Native Kubernetes CRDs for extending the policy library (aka “constraint templates”).
● Audit functionality.

Why you need Gatekeeper?

As a Platform DevOps engineer managing multiple Kubernetes clusters for various projects, you face challenges when users deploy applications without adhering to best practices. Key issues include:
  1.     Lack of ownership unless users apply labels themselves.
  2.     No resource limits, leading to high costs.
  3.     Untrusted images impacting other applications.
  4.     Security and best practice violations across clusters.

To address these, we need governance to enforce limits and rules via policies. OPA Gatekeeper can be configured to implement these policies, ensuring best practices are followed.

videoTitle: SBOM & Security Scanning in CI/CD | Secure Your DevOps Pipeline with GitHub Actions
videoDescription:
🚀 SBOM & Security Scanning in CI/CD | Secure Your DevOps Pipeline with GitHub Actions 🔒

In this video, we take CI/CD security to the next level by integrating SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generation and advanced security scanning into your GitHub Actions workflow. If you're only scanning container images for vulnerabilities, you're missing hidden risks! 😱

We’ll cover:
✅ What is SBOM & Why You Need It? 📜
✅ How to Generate an SBOM with Syft 🔍
✅ Scanning Dependencies for Vulnerabilities with Grype 🚨
✅ Performing Full Security Scans with Trivy 🛡
✅ Step-by-Step CI/CD Implementation using GitHub Actions

By the end of this video, you'll have an automated security pipeline that ensures complete supply chain security in your CI/CD workflows! 🔥

🔄 Hands-on Demo:
We'll walk through a real-world GitHub Actions pipeline that:
🔹 Generates an SBOM with Syft
🔹 Scans dependencies with Grype

videoTitle: Cracking the DevOps Code: Courses & Certifications for Success
videoDescription:
Take the courses mentioned here, get certifications and do not forget to add these using our AI-Powered Resume Builder (https://pro.kudoswall.com)
Certifications for Employed DevOps Professionals:
AWS Certified DevOps Engineer: This certification from Amazon Web Services (AWS) validates your expertise in deploying, operating, and managing applications on the AWS platform.
Azure DevOps Engineer: Offered by Microsoft, this certification demonstrates your skills in designing and implementing DevOps practices using Azure technologies.
Google Cloud Professional DevOps Engineer: This certification focuses on designing, developing, and managing DevOps practices on the Google Cloud Platform.
Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA): Certified by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), CKA validates your Kubernetes skills, which are crucial for container orchestration.
Certified Jenkins Engineer: Offered by CloudBees, this certification recognizes your proficiency in using Jenkins for continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines.
Red Hat Certified Engineer in DevOps: This certification from Red Hat validates your skills in using Red Hat technologies for automating, managing, and optimizing DevOps processes.

Courses for Aspiring DevOps Engineers:
Introduction to DevOps Practices: Online platforms like Coursera, edX, and Udemy offer various introductory courses that cover the fundamental concepts and practices of DevOps.
Docker and Kubernetes Courses: Understanding containerization and orchestration tools like Docker and Kubernetes is essential. There are numerous courses available for beginners and advanced users.
Cloud Platform Courses (AWS, Azure, GCP): Learning the basics of cloud platforms and their DevOps-related services is crucial. Many platforms offer free and paid courses for beginners.
CI/CD Pipeline Courses: Courses on continuous integration and continuous delivery tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, and CircleCI are essential for automating software delivery.
Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Courses: Learning tools like Terraform and Ansible for automating infrastructure deployment is key for DevOps engineers.
Monitoring and Logging Courses: Understanding tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) for monitoring and logging is vital.
Scripting and Programming Courses: Proficiency in scripting languages like Python and programming languages like Java can enhance your DevOps skills.
Security and Compliance Courses: DevOps engineers should also be well-versed in security practices. Courses on DevSecOps and security automation can be beneficial.
#DevOpsIndia #Certifications #Courses #TechSkills #CareerGrowth #Cloud #Automation

videoTitle: Day 27 – ARM Templates & Bicep with Azure DevOps | 30 Days Azure DevOps Challenge
videoDescription:
🚀 Welcome to Day 27 of my 30 Days Azure DevOps Challenge!

🔹 Topic: ARM Templates & Bicep with Azure DevOps

Yesterday, we learned how Terraform brings Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to multiple clouds.
Today, we’re focusing on Azure-native IaC tools:

👉 Key differences:

ARM Templates → JSON files that define Azure resources.

Bicep → A cleaner, simpler language to write the same infrastructure.

✅ Why use them?

Strong native Azure support

Seamless integration with Azure DevOps

Best fit for teams working mainly within Azure

✨ Key takeaway:

Terraform = multi-cloud flexibility 🌎

ARM/Bicep = deep Azure integration & simplicity 💡

📌 Next in Day 28, I’ll cover Monitoring & Logging in Azure DevOps — making sure your apps and pipelines run reliably!

videoTitle: RabbitMQ for DevOps Engineers: Hands-On Demo for Beginners #devops #rabbitmq  #kubernetes
videoDescription:
RabbitMQ for DevOps Engineers: Hands-On Demo for Beginners #devops #rabbitmq  #kubernetes

Github Repo : https://github.com/RohanRusta21/demo_rabbitmq.git

RabbitMQ Official Doc : https://www.rabbitmq.com/docs

Timestamp:
Understand Concept : 00:00
RabbitMQ Hands-on : 22:06

🧠 1. What Is RabbitMQ?

RabbitMQ is an open-source message broker — it acts as a middleman between different parts of a system that need to communicate asynchronously.
It uses the AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol) as its core protocol.

In simpler terms:

It receives messages from producers (applications sending data),

Stores them temporarily in queues,

And delivers them to consumers (applications receiving data).

This helps decouple systems — the sender and receiver don’t need to be active at the same time.

⚙️ 2. Why Use RabbitMQ?

✅ Decoupling – Producers don’t need to know who consumes messages.
✅ Asynchronous communication – Improves system responsiveness.
✅ Load balancing – Messages can be distributed across multiple consumers.
✅ Reliability – Ensures message delivery even if parts of the system fail.
✅ Scalability – You can add more queues or consumers easily.
✅ Flexible routing – Decide how messages flow using exchanges and routing keys.

🔩 3. Core RabbitMQ Concepts
🏭 Producers

Applications that send messages to RabbitMQ.
They don’t send messages directly to a queue but to an exchange.

🎯 Exchanges

An exchange routes messages to queues based on routing rules.

Exchange types:

Type Behavior Example Use Case
Direct Routes messages to queues whose binding key exactly matches routing key Task queues
Fanout Broadcasts all messages to all queues bound to it Notifications, pub/sub
Topic Routes messages based on pattern matching (* and #) Selective subscriptions
Headers Routes based on header attributes Advanced filtering

He highlights his passion for cloud computing and how AI is reshaping DevOps by streamlining automation, security analysis, and natural language-based cloud deployments.

###

* AI is enhancing DevOps
* Cloud cost optimization is in demand

* Start reading: https://allthingsopen.org/articles

It includes the All Things Open conference, the largest open source / tech / web event on the U.S. East Coast, meetups in the Research Triangle Park (RTP) of NC, South Carolina, and New York City, and a YouTube channel with more than 1,000 free recordings.
